authoritative_sources.title: Current Good Manufacturing Practices (CGMPs) for Food and Dietary Supplements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's food and dietary supplement CGMP overview is authoritative for the packaged-food baseline and explicitly ties manufacturers to Part 117 and category-specific CGMP regimes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baseline process, sanitation, and preventive-control framing

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR text is the primary legal baseline for current human-food manufacturing controls, implementation records, corrective actions, supply-chain programs, and record-retention requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control mapping, evidence review, corrective-action, and release-boundary handling
used_for: record retention and official review posture

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Industry: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FDA guidance directly addressing brand-owner, receiving-facility, and co-manufacturer supplier approval and verification obligations in packaged-food supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier verification and co-manufacturer boundary handling
used_for: handoff boundary between review support and final supplier approval

authoritative_sources.title: Food Traceability List
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA maintains the public list of higher-risk foods that trigger additional traceability record expectations, which is authoritative for covered-food category handling.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Intends to Extend Compliance Date for Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This March 20, 2025 FDA constituent update is the agency's public statement that it intends to extend the Food Traceability Rule compliance date beyond January 20, 2026, which affects timing-sensitive packaged-food control posture.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's final recall guidance is authoritative for recall-initiation preparations, communications, and responsibilities in distribution chains handling FDA-regulated goods.

authoritative_sources.title: Fresh Foods Standards in Use
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for product identification and lot-based traceability; this public guidance explains GTIN and batch-lot conventions used across packaged-goods traceability and recall workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: terminology normalization and traceability data handling

authoritative_sources.title: How to Conduct a Recall
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC recall guidance is authoritative for U.S. non-food consumer goods and helps define where consumer-goods product compliance must escalate into CPSC reporting and recall handling rather than assuming a food-only baseline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: non-food consumer-goods recall and reporting boundary

workflow_stages.name: intake and lane-boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm product family, facility, jurisdiction, systems of record, and authority map before classifying the request as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map packaged-goods compliance obligations, preventive controls, supplier program requirements, and tenant controls to the specific lot, supplier, and release context.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: evidence and traceability review
workflow_stages.description: Review batch, lot, inventory, shipment, supplier, and hold records to determine whether visible evidence supports the requested analysis or must remain blocked.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: exception, hold, and recall-readiness escalation
workflow_stages.description: Translate nonconformance, recall, traceability conflict, or release pressure into boundary-safe escalation packages without claiming release or legal authority.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: production orders and food safety plans
batch records, lot genealogy, GTIN or lot-code mappings, and inventory-state records
quality holds, nonconformance reports, CAPA or corrective-action records, and release records
supplier, co-manufacturer, importer, and verification records
traceability logs, consignee lists, and shipment or warehouse records
recall procedures, complaint trend summaries, and withdrawal or recall packets
tenant authority maps, policy overlays, and change-control documents

allowed: determine whether obligations, controls, and evidence requirements are satisfied, missing, stale, or in conflict
prepare evidence-backed control matrices, review packets, and exception escalation notes
classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
route exceptions, source conflicts, recall-readiness questions, and hold-release concerns to the correct owner
escalate: final release authority, quality disposition, or engineering decision is required
supplier approval, substitution, or rework authorization is requested
legal, regulator-facing, or policy-interpretation issues materially change the answer
tenant policy, product-category applicability, or source timing conflicts change the workflow outcome
refuse: bypass traceability, release, or quality controls
provide legal advice, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
treat incomplete or contradictory lot, inventory, or supplier evidence as sufficient for release or recall closure

domain_failure_modes: traceability records conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, and shipment records
product is released or shipped while quality-hold or corrective-action evidence remains unresolved
supplier verification or co-manufacturer evidence is missing but substitution or release pressure continues
public FDA or CPSC expectations conflict with tenant policy or product-category assumptions and the lane guesses instead of escalating
the lane drifts into formal legal interpretation, supplier approval, or final quality-release authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when required facts or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability Rule compliance-date timing for covered foods after FDA's March 20, 2025 extension announcement
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: FDA publicly announced on March 20, 2025 that it intends to extend the compliance date by 30 months, but the lane should still require tenant confirmation of current enforcement posture and covered-product applicability before making timing-sensitive conclusions.

notes: The public baseline is intentionally strongest for FDA-regulated packaged foods and adjacent CPG traceability workflows; tenant retrieval must add category-specific overlays for dietary supplements, cosmetics, OTC products, customer standards, and jurisdiction-specific duties when relevant.
CPSC recall guidance is included to keep the lane from overfitting to food-only workflows when the tenant sells non-food packaged goods.
